intro: The decrees of God are his eternal, sovereign purposes by which he foreordains all things that come to pass, including the salvation of his people. The concept appears throughout Scripture in various formulations: God's counsel stands forever and his plans from generation to generation (Psalm 33:11); his works were planned before the foundation of the world (Ephesians 1:4; 2 Thessalonians 2:13); and all things are known to him from eternity (Acts 15:18). Systematic theology distinguishes between God's decretive will (what he has determined will certainly occur) and his preceptive will (what he commands human beings to do), as well as his decrees of election, reprobation, creation, providence, and redemption. The doctrine of divine decrees has been a central and contested topic in Reformed and Arminian theology, with debates focusing on the relationship between divine sovereignty and human free will.
